title: 3 Establishing Policies for Ordering Currency
body_text:
3	Establishing Policies for Ordering Currency
3.1	Policy on Restricting Access to Cash Services
Each financial institution is responsible for designating its own Cash Services Users. It is recommended that financial institutions segregate duties and restrict administrative access to Cash Services. Practice a high level of due diligence when appointing Cash Services Users. Make sure that the permissions assigned to each user reflect current job responsibilities and authorization levels.
3.2	Policy on Internal Controls and Cash Services User Management
Your financial institution is responsible for employing security practices and establishing internal controls for monitoring Cash Services activity. It is recommended that internal controls and procedures relating to the use of Cash Services are established to prevent or detect errors in a timely manner. It is recommended that financial institution operations staff and external and internal auditors are vigilant regarding internal controls for Cash Services access.
Policies can include but are not limited to the following:
Ensuring that Cash Services Users have their own unique user IDs and donât share user IDs.
Ensuring that Cash Services Users receive their one-time Cash Services passwords in a secure and confidential way.
Cancelling access for Cash Services Users who are inactive for one year or more; no longer require Cash Services access; or are no longer employees of your financial institution.
Ensuring that Cash Services Users are reviewed on a regular basis and that required action is taken to update or cancel Cash Services profiles.
3.3	Policy on Client Centre Passwords
Cash Services Users access the application with password login on the Client Centre. Once the user is successfully authenticated, the Cash Services option will appear on the Client Centre Userâs applications page. Secure passwords are necessary to prevent unauthorized access to the computer network. See the User Management Part for more information.
3.4	Policy on Internal Controls for Currency Orders
Financial institutions are responsible for security practices and internal controls. Operations staff and external and internal auditors must establish internal controls for ordering, selling, and maintaining an inventory of Canadian and US currency.
Such policies can include but are not limited to the following:
Receiving and verifying currency shipments in joint custody.
Maintaining the vault register in joint custody.
Monitoring the inventory of currency to ensure that the number of physical items corresponds to the vault register.
Maintaining a log of currency issued to each branch, if applicable.
Reconciling currency orders daily to ensure shipments and deliveries are current.
END
